channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Edmonton Elks dominate Ottawa Redblacks with 40-17 win
item.description: Justin Rankin delivered a standout performance, scoring three touchdowns and contributing 107 rushing yards on 14 carries as the Edmonton Elks secured a decisive 40-17 victory over the struggling Otta...

item.title: BYD Atto 8 Plug-in Hybrid Offers Premium Experience in South Africa
item.description: The BYD Atto 8 plug-in hybrid, priced at R1.25 million, is making waves in the South African market by offering a premium driving experience that challenges traditional luxury car perceptions. This re...

item.title: Meta's AI Image Generator Sparks Privacy Concerns Over Public Instagram Photo Use
item.description: Meta has launched its AI image generator, Muse Image, which allows users to create new images by leveraging photos from public Instagram accounts. The feature, part of Meta's Superintelligence Labs, e...

item.title: Tanzania's Hospitals Perform Successful Heart Surgeries on Burundian Children, Marking Regional Medical Milestone
item.description: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital and Jakaya Kikwete Cardiac Institute have successfully performed life-saving heart surgeries on three Burundian children, marking Tanzania's first paediatric cardiac operations...

item.title: Vivo and Dixon Technologies Launch Joint Venture to Expand Smartphone Manufacturing in India
item.description: India has approved a joint venture between Chinese smartphone giant Vivo and local manufacturer Dixon Technologies, marking a significant step in the country's smartphone manufacturing growth. This pa...

item.title: Abdullahi Accuses APC Of Holding El-Rufai In Custody Until After 2027 Elections
item.description: The African Democratic Party (ADC) has accused the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC) of deliberately holding former Kaduna State Governor Nasir El-Rufai in custody until after the 2027 elections....

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay’s film 'Jana Nayagan' gets 'A' certificate after prolonged certification process
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and actor Vijay's film 'Jana Nayagan' has finally received an 'A' certificate from the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC) after a seven-month delay. Originally schedu...

item.title: Kyiv braces for escalating missile attacks from Russia
item.description: The article discusses the heightened tension in Kyiv as Russia intensifies its missile attacks on Ukrainian territory. Residents are expressing concern over the increasing frequency and scale of these...

item.title: Njoki Chege Appointed CEO of Christian Media Org to Expand Gospel Outreach
item.description: Njoki Chege, former Daily Nation columnist known for her provocative 'City Girl' column, has transitioned to leading Transworld Radio Kenya (TWR Kenya), a Christian media organisation. The CEO role in...

item.title: Health Minister Calls for Regulations on Unhealthy Food and Alcohol Marketing
item.description: Health Minister Dr Aaron Motsoaledi has highlighted the urgent need for stricter regulations on unhealthy food and alcohol marketing, warning that South Africa is facing a growing crisis of lifestyle ...

item.title: Boyle Heights Community Demands Answers After Lineage Warehouse Fire and Toxic Smoke Crisis
item.description: Residents of Boyle Heights, California, erupted in frustration and anger during a town hall meeting Thursday night, demanding accountability and transparency following the Lineage cold-storage warehou...

item.title: Huntington Beach Police Warn Against Potential Takeover Event Amid Concerns Over Criminal Activity
item.description: Huntington Beach police have issued warnings to online organizers and promoters of a potential 'takeover' event similar to Newport Beach's chaotic Fourth of July celebration, which led to widespread c...

item.title: Tanzania Urged to Boost Research Investments for Health Solutions
item.description: The Minister for Education, Science and Technology, Professor Adolf Mkenda, has emphasized the need for Tanzania to increase investment in scientific research to tackle health challenges. During a vis...

item.title: Punjab Police Investigate Death of 10-Year-Old Girl in Lahore Tuition Centre
item.description: A 10-year-old girl was discovered unconscious and later pronounced dead in the bathroom of a private tuition centre in Lahore’s Ichhra area. Police registered a murder case after her father, Muhammad ...

item.title: Pakistan and Bangladesh Strengthen Cybercrime Cooperation
item.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi of Pakistan and his Bangladeshi counterpart Salahuddin Ahmed have agreed to extend bilateral cooperation in combating cybercrime and online fraud. The ministers held a m...

item.title: France Advance to World Cup Semis After Defeating Morocco
item.description: France booked a FIFA World Cup semi-final spot after a 2-0 victory over Morocco in the quarter-finals, ending the Atlas Lions' tournament campaign. Morocco, seeking historic success after becoming the...

item.title: APC Leaders Unite to Resolve Oyo State Crisis Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Prominent APC leaders in Oyo State, including former Senate Leader Teslim Folarin, Minister Adebayo Adelabu, and Senator Abdulfatai Buhari, held a closed-door meeting to address internal party conflic...

item.title: Extradition Proceedings Begin for Three B.C. Men Linked to Transnational Drug Trafficking Network
item.description: Extradition proceedings have commenced in Vancouver's B.C. Supreme Court for three British Columbia men accused of leading a transnational drug trafficking network. Ravinder Dhanda, 57, Lawrence Bishn...

item.title: Journalist's AI Prompt Deleted, Segal vs SBS, and eSafety's Senate Estimates Prep
item.description: The article explores controversies surrounding AI-generated content in journalism, focusing on a deleted paragraph from a Marie Claire Australia piece about Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. It highlig...

item.title: Former SA Air Force General Sentenced for Foreign Agent Activities
item.description: Former South African Air Force Brigadier General Portia 'Posh' Anyamba has been sentenced to six months in a U.S. jail after pleading guilty to acting as a foreign agent for South Africa. The plea agr...
